revision.text:
[[ceb:Benten-jima (pulo sa Hapon, lat 43,34, long 145,58)]][[ja:弁天島 (根室市)]][[ko:벤텐섬 (네무로시)]] {{Pathnav|Japan|Hokkaido|Nemuro|frame=1|qq=}} {{Infobox island |name = Bentenjima |image = |caption = |coordinates = |latitude_deg = 43 |latitude_min = 20 |latitude_sec = 32 |longitude_deg = 145 |longitude_min = 34 |longitude_sec = 35 |area = |perimeter = |elevation = 13 |highest_point = |largest_city = |archipelago = |waters = {{ill|Nemuro Bay|en|Nemuro Bay|qid=Q5355884|lt=Nemuro Bay|1=Nemuro Bay}} |country = {{JPN|qq=}} {{ill|Hokkaido|en|Hokkaido|qid=Q35581|lt=Hokkaido|1=Hokkaido}} |map = |map2 = Japan Hokkaido#Japan }} [[File:Nemuro city center area Aerial photograph.1978.jpg|thumb|300px|The long, narrow island at the top of the image is Bentenjima. This image is oriented northwest. Taken in 1978, it is a composite of 9 photographs.
{{ill|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|en|Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|qid=Q2986578|lt=Geospatial Information Authority of Japan|1=Geospatial Information Authority of Japan}}.]] '''Bentenjima''' (弁天島) is an {{ill|uninhabited island|en|uninhabited island|qid=Q1065592|lt=uninhabited island|1=Uninhabited island}} in {{ill|Nemuro Port|ja|根室港|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nemuro Port|1=Port of Nemuro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11536272}}, located in {{ill|Nemuro, Hokkaido|en|Nemuro, Hokkaido|qid=Q502205|lt=Nemuro|1=Nemuro, Hokkaido}}, {{ill|Hokkaido|en|Hokkaido|qid=Q35581|lt=Hokkaido|1=Hokkaido}}.{{refnest|group="Note"|Another island named "Bentenjima" exists in the northern part of Nemuro City. It is part of the {{ill|Habomai Islands|ja|歯舞諸島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habomai Islands|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歯舞群島|1=Habomai Islands|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1050542}} within the {{ill|Northern Territories|ja|北方地域|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Northern Territories|1=South Kuril Islands|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11401819}} and lies north of {{ill|Polonsky Island|en|Polonsky Island|qid=Q904875|lt=Taraku Island|1=Polonsky Island}}.|qq=}} In {{ill|Ainu language|en|Ainu language|qid=Q27969|lt=Ainu language|1=Ainu language}}, the island is called "Horomoshiri," meaning "large island." During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, it was known to the {{ill|Japanese people|ja|和人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese people|1=Wajin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11417139}} as "Daikokujima" (大黒島). {{ill|Takadaya Kahei|en|Takadaya Kahei|qid=Q4450102|lt=Takadaya Kahei|1=Takadaya Kahei}} built a shrine to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁才天}} on the island, which led to its current name. The entire island is an important site for {{ill|Okhotsk culture|en|Okhotsk culture|qid=Q1093651|lt=Okhotsk culture|1=Okhotsk culture}} archaeological remains. Additionally, the oldest lighthouse in Hokkaido was built here in 1872 (Meiji 5), although it no longer exists. == Geography == Bentenjima is approximately 600 meters west of {{ill|Kotohira, Kagawa|en|Kotohira, Kagawa|qid=Q1271483|lt=Kotobira Town|1=Kotohira, Kagawa}}, Nemuro City, and about 200 meters northwest of Nemuro Port. The island has an area of 0.05 km² and a perimeter of 1.2 km. The northern and western breakwaters of Nemuro Port extend from the island, and the island itself serves as part of the port's breakwater. == History == === Bentenjima Shell Midden === In 1878 (Meiji 11), {{ill|John Milne|en|John Milne|qid=Q2739759|lt=John Milne|1=John Milne}}, a foreign archaeologist hired by the Japanese government, discovered a shell midden on Bentenjima. Like the {{ill|Moyoro Shell Mound|en|Moyoro Shell Mound|qid=Q11344562|lt=Moyoro Shell Midden|1=Moyoro Shell Mound}} in {{ill|Abashiri|en|Abashiri|qid=Q305640|lt=Abashiri|1=Abashiri}}, it belongs to the Okhotsk culture. The entire island is a shell midden containing {{ill|pit dwellings|ja|竪穴建物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit dwellings|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竪穴式住居|qq=Pit-house|2=ja|3=竪穴式住居|1=Pit-house|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q940435}} and a large quantity of animal bones. While the site is significant for studying Okhotsk culture, its importance has been recognized more internationally than domestically. Further excavations continued, and a major investigation by {{ill|Tokyo University of Education|ja|東京教育大学|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo University of Education|1=Tokyo University of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11524880}} (the predecessor of {{ill|University of Tsukuba|en|University of Tsukuba|qid=Q743615|lt=University of Tsukuba|1=University of Tsukuba}}) was conducted in 1962 (Showa 37). This research unearthed remains of {{ill|Brown bear|en|Brown bear|qid=Q36341|lt=brown bears|1=Brown bear}}, {{ill|Yezo sika deer|en|Yezo sika deer|qid=Q11290159|lt=Yezo deer|1=Yezo sika deer}}, seals, and whales, along with stone tools. These findings suggest that the {{ill|Okhotsk people|ja|オホーツク人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okhotsk people|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オホーツク文化|1=Okhotsk culture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1093651}} engaged in hunting marine and terrestrial animals. === Laxman's Visit to Nemuro === In 1782 (Tenmei 2), {{ill|Daikokuya Kōdayū|en|Daikokuya Kōdayū|qid=Q2563388|lt=Daikokuya Kodayu|1=Daikokuya Kōdayū}}, a merchant from {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|qid=Q864166|lt=Ise Province|1=Ise Province}}, was shipwrecked near the {{ill|Aleutian Islands|en|Aleutian Islands|qid=Q119285|lt=Aleutian Islands|1=Aleutian Islands}}. He was rescued by {{ill|Adam Laxman|en|Adam Laxman|qid=Q29787|lt=Adam Laxman|1=Adam Laxman}} and eventually taken to {{ill|Saint Petersburg|en|Saint Petersburg|qid=Q656|lt=Saint Petersburg|1=Saint Petersburg}}, where he met Empress {{ill|Catherine the Great|ja|エカチェリーナ2世|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Catherine the Great|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to エカチェリーナ2世 (ロシア皇帝)|1=Catherine the Great|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q36450}}. In 1792 (Kansei 4), Laxman, accompanied by Kodayu, visited Nemuro Port as an envoy. Laxman's group anchored at Bentenjima, with 42 members disembarking to spend eight months in Nemuro. During the winter, they skated on the frozen bay, marking the first recorded instance of ice skating in Japan. === Benzaiten and Ichikishima Shrine === In the {{ill|Culture|en|Culture|qid=Q11042|lt=Bunka|1=Culture}}, Kobayashi Kyuhei established a shrine to Benzaiten on Bentenjima. This shrine was destroyed in 1843 (Tenpo 14) due to a fire. It was rebuilt in 1865 (Genji 2), with costs covered by Fujino Kihei. In 1882 (Meiji 15), the shrine was designated a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}. Today, the shrine is known as {{ill|Ichikishima Shrines|lt=Ichikishima Shrines|lt=Ichikishima Shrine|ja|市杵島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima Shrine|qq=}}, and its annual festival is held on September 15. While the island is typically off-limits, visitors may access it during the festival via ferries from Nemuro Port. === Matsura Takeshiro's Records === The explorer {{ill|Matsuura Takeshirō|en|Matsuura Takeshirō|qid=Q11531839|lt=Matsura Takeshiro|1=Matsuura Takeshirō}} documented Bentenjima in his "Shiretoko Diaries." He noted that the island was called "Horomoshiri" ("large island") by the {{ill|Ainu people|ja|アイヌ人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ainu people|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アイヌ|1=Ainu people|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q101828}} and reported its dimensions as "about 550 meters offshore and 980 meters in circumference." During this period, it was also known as "Daikokujima" by the Japanese. === Hokkaido's Oldest Lighthouse === The challenging navigation conditions of Nemuro Bay and surrounding waters necessitated the construction of lighthouses. The first lighthouse in Hokkaido was lit on Bentenjima in Meiji 5 (1872). The original structure was destroyed in the 1945 Hokkaido air raids. === Lindbergh's Visit === In 1931, {{ill|Charles Lindbergh|en|Charles Lindbergh|qid=Q1618|lt=Charles Lindbergh|1=Charles Lindbergh}} crossed the Pacific, stopping at Bentenjima before landing at Nemuro Port. == Location == * Address: Bentenjima, Nemuro, Hokkaido, Japan * Postal Code: 087-0000 * Coordinates: 43°20'25"N, 145°34'50"E == Notes == == References == {{Reflist|colwidth=30em|qq=}} == See also == * {{ill|Nemuro Peninsula Chashi Sites|en|Nemuro Peninsula Chashi Sites|qid=Q11110966|lt=Nemuro Peninsula Chashi Sites|1=Nemuro Peninsula Chashi Sites}} * {{ill|1st Regional Coast Guard Headquarters|ja|第一管区海上保安本部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1st Regional Coast Guard Headquarters|qq=}} {{Munakata Faith|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|弁天島 (根室市)|version=95534528|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:弁天島 (根室市)]] [[ceb:Benten-jima (pulo sa Hapon, lat 43,34, long 145,58)]] [[ja:弁天島 (根室港)]] [[ko:벤텐섬 (네무로시)]] [[ja:%E5%BC%81%E5%A4%A9%E5%B3%B6 (%E6%A0%B9%E5%AE%A4%E5%B8%82)]] {{wikidata link|Q11486910}} [[Category:オホーツク文化]] [[Category:Uninhabited islands of Hokkaido]] [[Category:根室市の地理]] [[Category:根室市の建築物]] [[Category:根室市の歴史]] [[Category:根室市の町・字]]
